attacking_style: Launches decisive solo attacks from breakaways on selective, punchy finishes in final kilometers. Targets final climbs with enough gradient to shed sprinters but not sustained enough for pure climbers.
team_tactics: Bahrain grants him freedom for stage hunting when significantly down on GC (15+ minutes). Operates independently in breakaways rather than relying on team support in final.
terrain_preference: Selective finishes with short, punchy final climbs (Signal d'Uchon-type). Strong enough to survive hilly breakaway formations with 25-30 riders. Exposed on sustained mountain stages requiring pure climbing.
vulnerability: Crashes have disrupted preparation/form earlier in stage races. Requires GC freedom which only comes after significant time loss. Cannot compete with pure climbers on longer ascents.
key_patterns: Wins from large breakaways (29 riders), attacking solo on final climb. 2 observations.
Most dangerous when 15+ minutes down on GC, granted stage-hunting freedom by Bahrain. 2 observations.
Targets selective punchy finishes rather than summit finishes or flat sprints. 2 observations.
form_trajectory: positive: Demonstrated continued threat status in 2022 Tour breakaways despite earlier crashes, suggesting resilience and maintained selective-terrain climbing strength
